prompt: A powerful conceptual image showing invisible inequality in air pollution. A single urban street scene divided asymmetrically with no visible barrier. On one side, the air is clear, soft natural light, open space, and people moving freely. On the other side, the air is dense with heavy particulate haze, visibility reduced, light appears thick and muted, and people move slowly with subtle strain as if breathing is difficult. The polluted side feels heavy and compressed, while the clean side feels open and breathable. Strong cinematic composition, deep atmospheric contrast, emotional tone of unease and imbalance, minimal background clutter, no text, no symbols, no charts, no maps, no globes, no numbers, no infographic elements. size : 1024*1024
insight: Air pollution exposure is uneven, with some populations living in conditions that feel visibly heavier and harder to breathe than others.
